title: Scalar throughput: example.multiply(n, 2)
description: Per-row scalar RPC throughput across N rows. Measures how many scalar calls per second VGI can sustain.
setup_sql: CREATE OR REPLACE TEMP TABLE bench_input AS SELECT i::BIGINT AS n FROM range(${param:rows}) t(i);

payload.notes: Scalars parallelize via the VGI C++ thread fanout — each DuckDB scan thread gets its own worker connection.

notes: Uses sum() to FORCE per-row evaluation. The naive count(*) of a SELECT-with-projection lets DuckDB elide the scalar call entirely (count doesn't reference the column). Per-row: 16 bytes in (2x BIGINT), 8 bytes out.
